channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-1190] Test failure on test suite sanity, subtest test_51c
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for sarah <sarah@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/5c47b28a-66fd-11e1-a9a4-5254004bbbd3.

The sub-test test_51c failed with the following error:

test_51c failed with 2

Info required for matching: sanity 51c
item.summary: Test failure on test suite sanity, subtest test_51c

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: reopening to assign fix version 2.3

Integrated in lustre-dev » x86_64,client,el6,inkernel #340
LU-1190 test: Test failure on sanity, subtest test_51c. (Revision 30fd56b12ec22b3e19298c1f7e7c5f54551c015b)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : 30fd56b12ec22b3e19298c1f7e7c5f54551c015b

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,server,el5,ofa #513
LU-1190 test: Test failure on sanity, subtest test_51c. (Revision 30fd56b12ec22b3e19298c1f7e7c5f54551c015b)

Result = SUCCESS
Oleg Drokin : 30fd56b12ec22b3e19298c1f7e7c5f54551c015b
Files :

  • lustre/tests/sanity.sh

The landing of LU-993 was breaking every sanity.sh test run for master (with SLOW=yes) since it was landed. It is important to try and catch/fix this kind of issue earlier (it took a month to find and fix this simple problem). Otherwise, the confidence in the test system decreases, and people (including myself) tend to assume that test failures can be ignored.

This test failure was introduced by LU-993 landing, because test_51c() is only run with SLOW=yes, and the newly added test_51ba() was deleting all of the subdirectories that test_51c() was also expecting to delete.
verified, pass: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/bc516a2e-69bc-11e1-9d76-5254004bbbd3
Patch unload to:http://review.whamcloud.com/2269
Yangsheng will look into this one
